content:
# Approved Supplier Register

**Tool ID:** T015-01
**Block:** B015 (Supplier Due Diligence)
**Domain:** Food Safety & Hygiene (D01_food_safety)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Tracker
**Role:** Manager
**Time Required:** 5-10 minutes per entry; 30 minutes initial setup
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is a foundational tool -- the kind that prevents problems before they start. It is designed to be picked up and used immediately, no training required.

What gets measured gets managed. Under the Food Safety Act 1990, you must demonstrate due diligence in choosing and monitoring your suppliers. This register is your evidence that every supplier has been assessed, approved, and is regularly reviewed. If contaminated food reaches a customer and you cannot show due diligence in your supply chain, legal liability falls directly on you.

**Built for Manager working in Food Safety & Hygiene.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Central register listing every approved supplier with company details, products supplied, food safety credentials, documentation held, approval date, and next review date. This is your due diligence evidence for EHO inspections and your reference point for supplier management.

This tool supports **Supplier Due Diligence** (B015) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Manager (creates and maintains)
**Time Required:** 30 minutes for initial setup; 5 minutes per new supplier entry
**Frequency:** Updated when any new supplier is added; reviewed annually for all suppliers

**What You'll Need:**
- This register (digital spreadsheet recommended for ease of updating)
- Supplier contact details, certificates, and insurance documents
- Food safety certificates from each supplier (e.g., SALSA, BRC, ISO 22000)
- Allergen specification sheets for supplied products
- 30 minutes to compile information for existing suppliers

**Steps:**
1. List every supplier you currently buy from
2. For each supplier, gather and record the details in the register below
3. Request any missing documentation from suppliers
4. Set review dates (annually for each supplier, or sooner if issues arise)
5. Do NOT order from any supplier not on this register
6. When a new supplier is proposed, complete the New Supplier Questionnaire (T015-03) before adding them

---

## Tracking Log

### Approved Supplier Register

| # | Supplier Name | Contact Name | Phone | Email | Products Supplied | Food Safety Cert | Cert Expiry | Allergen Specs on File | Liability Insurance | Approved Date | Next Review | Status |

| 1 | *e.g., Origin Coffee Roasters* | *J. Smith* | *01onal 555 0101* | *orders@origin.co.uk* | *Coffee beans (house blend, single origin, decaf)* | *SALSA* | *Dec 2026* | *☐ Yes* | *☐ Yes* | *Jan 2026* | *Jan 2027* | *Active* |
| 2 | *e.g., County Dairy* | *M. Jones* | *01onal 555 0202* | *sales@countydairy.co.uk* | *Whole milk, semi-skimmed, oat milk, butter, cream* | *BRC Grade A* | *Mar 2027* | *☐ Yes* | *☐ Yes* | *Jan 2026* | *Jan 2027* | *Active* |
| 3 | *e.g., Artisan Bakery Co* | *S. Williams* | *01onal 555 0303* | *trade@artisanbakery.co.uk* | *Sourdough, croissants, pain au chocolat, scones* | *SALSA* | *Jun 2026* | *☐ Yes* | *☐ Yes* | *Feb 2026* | *Feb 2027* | *Active* |
| 4 | *e.g., Green Valley Produce* | *L. Brown* | *01onal 555 0404* | *hello@gvproduce.co.uk* | *Salad, tomatoes, avocado, fruit, vegetables* | *Red Tractor* | *Sep 2026* | *N/A (whole produce)* | *☐ Yes* | *Mar 2026* | *Mar 2027* | *Active* |
| 5 | *e.g., Metro Catering Supplies* | *Trade Desk* | *0800 555 0505* | *orders@metrocatering.co.uk* | *Cleaning chemicals, disposables, packaging, labels* | *N/A (non-food)* | *N/A* | *N/A* | *☐ Yes* | *Jan 2026* | *Jan 2027* | *Active* |
| 6 | | | | | | | | ☐ Yes ☐ No | ☐ Yes ☐ No | | | Active / Under Review / Suspended |
| 7 | | | | | | | | ☐ Yes ☐ No | ☐ Yes ☐ No | | | Active / Under Review / Suspended |
| 8 | | | | | | | | ☐ Yes ☐ No | ☐ Yes ☐ No | | | Active / Under Review / Suspended |
| 9 | | | | | | | | ☐ Yes ☐ No | ☐ Yes ☐ No | | | Active / Under Review / Suspended |
| 10 | | | | | | | | ☐ Yes ☐ No | ☐ Yes ☐ No | | | Active / Under Review / Suspended |

### Documentation Checklist (per supplier)

For each approved supplier, confirm you hold the following:

| Document | Supplier 1 | Supplier 2 | Supplier 3 | Supplier 4 | Supplier 5 |
|----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|
| Food safety certificate (SALSA/BRC/ISO) | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ |
| Product specification sheets | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ |
| Allergen information for all products | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ |
| Public liability insurance certificate | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ |
| Delivery temperature records available | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ |
| Recall/withdrawal procedure documented | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ | ☐ |

### Annual Review Schedule

| Supplier | Review Due | Reviewed By | Review Outcome | Notes |
|----------|-----------|------------|---------------|-------|
| | | | ☐ Approved / ☐ Conditional / ☐ Suspended | |
| | | | ☐ Approved / ☐ Conditional / ☐ Suspended | |
| | | | ☐ Approved / ☐ Conditional / ☐ Suspended | |

### Review Notes

| Reviewed By | Date | Key Findings | Next Steps |
|------------|------|-------------|------------|
| | | | |

**Tip:** If a supplier has 3+ delivery temperature incidents (logged on T002-03) in any quarter, trigger an immediate review regardless of the annual schedule.

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Using a Supplier Who Has Never Been Formally Assessed

**Why it happens:** A friend recommends a local bakery. The Manager starts ordering from them because the products are good and the price is right. No food safety checks, no allergen specs, no insurance verification. If that bakery supplies a product containing an undeclared allergen, your cafe is liable.
**How to prevent:** No supplier orders until they are on this register. For new suppliers, complete the New Supplier Questionnaire (T015-03) first. This takes 20 minutes and could save the business.
**If it is already happening:** Audit all current suppliers this week. For any supplier not formally assessed, request their food safety certification and allergen specs immediately. If they cannot provide them, find an alternative supplier.

### Risk 2: Supplier Certificates Expire Without Anyone Noticing

**Why it happens:** The bakery was BRC-certified when you started ordering from them 3 years ago. Their certificate expired 18 months ago and they did not renew. You are still ordering from them, assuming they are certified.
**How to prevent:** Record expiry dates in the register. Set calendar reminders 1 month before each expiry. Contact the supplier to confirm renewal. If they have not renewed, ask why and assess whether to continue.
**If it is already happening:** Check every supplier certificate expiry date today. Contact any supplier whose certificate has expired. If they are no longer certified, discuss with the Owner whether to continue the relationship.

### Risk 3: Register Exists but Is Never Used for Procurement Decisions

**Why it happens:** The register is completed as a compliance document and filed. Meanwhile, procurement decisions are made informally -- the Kitchen Lead orders from whoever has the best price this week, regardless of whether they are on the approved list.
**How to prevent:** Make the register the single point of truth for procurement. All staff who place orders must check that the supplier is on the register and their status is "Active." Unauthorized supplier orders require Manager approval before they are placed.
**If it is already happening:** Review the last month of deliveries. Cross-check every supplier against the register. Flag any that are not listed. Either add them (after completing T015-03) or stop ordering from them.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** You may have 5-8 suppliers. That is a manageable register. The value is in having the documentation in one place, especially allergen specs -- these are critical for your Allergen Matrix (T005-01).
**Chain / Franchise:** Your brand likely mandates approved suppliers centrally. Use this register for any local or supplementary suppliers not covered by the corporate list.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Even with 3-4 suppliers, this register matters. Your milk supplier and your food supplier both need food safety credentials on file.
**Bakery Cafe:** Pay special attention to flour, sugar, and ingredient suppliers. Allergen specs for baking ingredients are especially critical given the number of allergens in baked goods.

**Making it yours:**
- Replace the example suppliers with your actual supply chain
- Request all missing documentation this week
- Set calendar reminders for every review date
- Keep the register digital for easy updating, with a printed backup in the SFBB folder
- Review whenever a new supplier is considered or an existing supplier changes their product range

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T015-02 -- Delivery Inspection Checklist (L1, checklist)
- T015-03 -- New Supplier Questionnaire (L2, assessment)
- T015-04 -- Supplier Due Diligence SOP (L2, SOP)
- T015-05 -- Quarterly Supplier Performance Review (L3, scorecard)

**Related blocks:**

**Parent block:** B015 -- Supplier Due Diligence
**Module:** M01: Food Safety & Hygiene Compliance
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
